mnemonics: NB for Sentiment = Never Believe Negative
keyPoints: A Bayes Classifier uses Bayes' theorem to classify data into predefined categories.
In the Naive Bayes classifier, each feature is assumed to be independent of each other.
For sentiment analysis, we can use Naive Bayes to optimize the classification by focusing on the most important words and their contexts.
Naive Bayes can be used for other text classification tasks such as spam detection, genre classification, and topic modeling.
Naive Bayes can also be used as a language model to predict the probability of a word or phrase given a context.
